_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
_note: NVDA fiscal year ends January. FY2026 = Feb 2025 – Jan 2026. Use most recent TTM.
story_narrative: NVIDIA is the demand signal for the entire AI build-out. Blackwell GPU architecture dominates AI training (H100 → H200 → B100/B200) and is expanding into inference. The AI hardware stack requires NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem — 10+ years of software lock-in that AMD/Intel cannot easily replicate. Revenue went from $27B in FY2023 to $130B+ in FY2025 — a 5x in two years driven by hyperscaler CapEx. The risk: the current level of AI CapEx ($300B+ across hyperscalers in 2025) may not be sustainable; any deceleration hits NVDA disproportionately. Custom silicon (Marvell/Broadcom ASICs) may take share in inference workloads over time. The moat is real but the valuation at current prices embeds optimistic assumptions.
story_revenue_cagr: 22% from a very high base — hyperscaler AI CapEx continues to expand, Blackwell/Rubin ramp adds new product cycles. Conservative relative to consensus; bull case is 35%+.
story_target_margin: 55% EBIT margin reflects NVIDIA's extraordinary pricing power and software-defined CUDA moat. Current margins are 60%+; some normalization expected as competition grows.
story_growth_years: 10 years covers Blackwell → Rubin → next GPU architecture. Each cycle ~2 years; 10 years = ~5 GPU generations.
story_stable_growth: 3% — in mature state, NVIDIA grows with data center capex + new markets (automotive, robotics). Slower than current but well above GDP.
story_stable_margin: 38% stable EBIT — as competition intensifies in inference ASICs and AMD closes the gap, pricing power moderates. Still premium vs. peers.
story_s2c_high: 3.0x — NVIDIA is fabless (manufactures at TSMC). Incremental revenue requires almost no physical capital — only R&D and software investment. High S2C is the source of extreme FCF generation.
story_s2c_stable: 2.5x — slight decrease as NVIDIA invests in new compute platforms (DGX Cloud, NIM inference) that require more infrastructure capital.
story_beta: 1.75 — NVIDIA is a high-growth, cyclically-exposed tech company. AI CapEx sensitivity means a 10% reduction in hyperscaler budgets hits NVDA revenue 30%+.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. GPU architecture IP (Blackwell, Rubin) has a 2-year competitive lead cycle, but cumulative software/CUDA IP amortizes over 5 years.
story_uncertainty: High — extraordinary growth embeds future expectations; any CapEx deceleration or custom ASIC substitution would reprice significantly. Wide MoS required.
